description: Amazfit Bip U Pro Smartwatch is designed to give consumers an attractive alternative to today's pricey smartwatches. Boasting a 225mAh battery to deliver up to 9 days of endurance on a single charge. It also has 60+ sports modes with high-precision GPS letting you exercise at a higher level. With 5 ATM water resistance, this fitness tracker can resist any of your daily activities even swimming. Come with multiple health tracking features include 24/7 heart rate monitoring, sleep quality & stress level monitoring, menstrual cycle tracking, and blood-oxygen-level measurement, allowing you to better understand your health. With a 1.43‘’ large HD color screen and 50 watch faces to choose from, the Amazfit Bip U Pro fitness watch matches seamlessly your personal style. Amazon Alexa Built-In, Compatible with Android 5.0 or iOS 10.0 and above devices.
features: 【ALEXA & GPS BUILT-IN】You can talk to Amazon Alexa on your Amazfit Bip U Pro smartwatch, Ask questions, get translations, check the weather, control your smart home devices and more. And get precise tracking of your distance, daily step count thanks to integrated GPS..Water resistance depth:50 meters.Suported Application:Alarm,Breath Monitor,Calendar,Calorie Tracker,Heart Rate Monitor. Connectivity technology:GPS. Wireless comm standard:Bluetooth

【60plus SPORTS MODES & 5 ATM WATER RESISTANT】Featuring 60plus sports modes to help you exercise at a higher level. Water-resistant upto 50 meters, you can wear the fitness watch for swimming and it will record your achievements even underwater.

【MORE HEALTH TRACKING METRICS】The Amazfit Bip U Pro smart watch can provide all-around health tracking, allowing you to accurately track 24-hour heart rate, blood oxygen levels, stress levels, sleep quality, and female menstrual cycle.
【LONG 9-DAY BATTERY LIFE】When fully charged you can relax and enjoy more than a full week of travel or work without worrying about chargers.

【DESIGN FOR ALL-DAY COMFORT】Amazfit Bip U Pro fitness tracker only weighs 31 grams, An ultra-light design you can barely feel when striving for peak performance. ​Featuring a 1.43” large color screen with 320x302 pixel resolution, creates an exquisite visual display, making all incoming calls, messages, and reminders extremely clear.

【SMART NOTIFICATIONS】Get an intelligent little pal to remind you of the incoming calls, text messages, apps, and calendars, alarm clock, weather forecast, and suport controlling the music and camera of your smartphone.

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price.
reviews.text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

reviews.title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
reviews.text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

reviews.title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
reviews.text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

reviews.title: Did not like this nearly as much as my original amazfit bip watch
reviews.text:
I bought an Amazfit Bip watch a year or more ago and have really been enjoying it The app tracks all my cycling trips and the sleep monitoring is useful. Iam really a big fan of the long battery life of the older model.

This watch has a 9 day battery life which is not nearly as long as the 30 days of the original watch.

This watch meausres blood oxygen levels but taking the measurement requires holding your wrist it the right position and takes much longer that the finger tip device Ihave.

I did not exhaustively evaluate the new app that came with this phone but it did not look nearly as good as the original app.

reviews.title: Great Smart Watch
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:f32a02f232a3ca8c266f05f3ed09b052]] This is a great buy. If you want a smart watch that does not cost the big bucks this watch is it. I bought it for my husband for counting steps. He also has the advantage of all other functions such as heart rate, blood pressure, blood oxygen. When he went to doctors all functions matched what the doctor took. He came home really impressed. So, if you need a smart watch, the Cakuja Smart Watch is a great buy. Oh, it also connects to your smart phone and you can make and receive phone calls. I've tried it. We are now a Cakuja family.
Added video, still getting used to all functions.

title: Kimnix Smart Watch 2022(Make Call Receive/Dial), 1.72 in HD Full Touch Screen Fitness Tracker with Bluetooth Call/Text/Heart Rate/SpO2/Sleep Monitor Smartwatch for Android iOS Mens Women (Black)

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: It does not keep all of the apps current. That is what I bought it for.
reviews.text: I bought this watch per my doctors request to keep health issues.  I only use it for this feature.  But to my amazement, It does not do as it states.  As I use only the medical aspects only I would expect it to work perfectly.

reviews.title: Finally a comfortable stretchy band
reviews.text: This watch band, that I have for my FitBit Inspire 2 is just what I was looking for. I hated the band that came with it.  I tried another buckle, cloth band, which was ok, but still a challenge to take on and off of my wrist.  This band is easy to attach to the Fit bit watch, and the length is adjustable (which I think is unusual for a slip-on stretchy band.  I even am ok with the multi color.  It makes it easy to pair up with lots of tops.  It can be adjusted in length to most everyone's wrist.  I have a very small wrist (short woman here) and I was able to make it small enough for me. But there is also a lot of room for expansion.

reviews.title: Great looking watch, battery life isn't what I expected
reviews.text: The major feature that seems missing from this watch is sleep tracking.  Reviews of the watch point to the limited battery life of the watch, and not being enough to track though an average night's sleep.

reviews.title: Measure your wrist before you buy.
reviews.text:
The blue silicone band was too small to go around my wrist. I called Skagen and they offered to sell me a bigger band. I told them I shouldn't have to pay extra for the watch to go on my wrist. They said since I bought it from Amazon there was nothing they could do. So sadly, this is getting returned.
I've worn plenty of watches before, and I don't have a fatty wrist. My wrist is 8 inches in circumference, which is average for a male.
The interesting thing about the size as I inspected and measured the watch, is that if you measure the watch laid out flat, 8 inches goes to the second to last notch, and it should fit fine. BUT -- the watch body itself obviously does not bend, which means in actual wear when the watch goes AROUND your wrist, you need an additional 3/4 to 1 inch for it to work.
It's basically like that one gallon jug I bought at Walmart, I got it home to make a drink mix in it just to realize that yes, it held one gallon, if you filled it to the absolute brim, at which point putting in the drink mix made it spill.
If your wrist is greater than 7 inches in circumference, I would not buy this watch.

reviews.title: I wanted to love it
reviews.text: It really isn't the watches fault that I returned it.  It did all it said it would do, the Alexa feature worked, the heart rate monitor worked , the picture was bright, among other things. I didn't like that if you use the timer ( which I use frequently at my job) you can't go back to the main screen, if you do it turns off the timer, but you can use Alexa as a timer and don't have to stay on the same screen so there is a work around. I was also dissapointed with the fact there were only 4 face choices.  But, the reason I returned it is that my phone is older and it wears down my battery quickly and was causing cpu (?) overload warnings.

reviews.title: Solid watch
reviews.text:
Watch came well packaged and was easy to set up and pair using the provided instructions on the item page. It was made well and kept good time. reviews.title: Great activity tracker with AMAZING Battery Life.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:e0fda475d57018948d4bdf5116c091bf]] I had a different version activity watch that was way more expensive to match my phone. For the cost this watch does just about everything I need it to for way less money.

A few things I love about the watch...the Battery Life. OMG the battery life! It lasted me a full week with wearing it to bed most nights, through multiple exercise walks and a few work outs, and hundreds of notifications from my messaging apps and phone calls. It's easy to clear the notifications I receive on the watch. I love that it tracks my sleep, heart rate, and stress levels easily. I also tested out in my pool for swimming and it was waterproof without having to shake the watch or turn on any special modes to keep it watertight.

I haven't figured out of it auto detects a work out but that could be because I obviously don't work out that much.

I tested it against my health app on my phone and they were pretty accurate against each other info steps and mileage. I can't figure out how to get the watch and the app to register my GPS coordinates to see the map of my walks but I could be missing the settings.

The app that gives the data is easy to navigate and update any settings. Seems very similar to other track laps I've used.
A few things I'm not liking about this watch...
I feel like the band and the watch itself that comes with the watch is a little too big for my wrist. And I have a bigger wrist for a female at my size. The watch face is bigger than some of my friends apple watches and taller than my old Samsung gear fit watch. I can SEE notifications on the watch but I can't respond or give a quick response to any messages. For Whatsapp or Facebook Messenger I wouldn't expect that but for SMS messages I would like to be able to give a quick text back either by speaking a response or by selecting from some quick options.

Those are really my only 2 complaints. I like this watch and will continue to use!

reviews.title: Such a great Apple Watch dupe!
reviews.text: This smart watch works great! It’s also so cute and the band has lots of holes for adjusting. The only difference I see that’s noticeable is the watch band is a flimsier rubber. But it also makes it feel more comfortable than my Apple Watch. Would make a great gift as well!

reviews.title: Did the job for a week, then it drowned
reviews.text: I charged the watch out of the box.  Followed instructions and setup was pretty much straightforward.  There is a decent amount of sports mode available.  I mainly use run, walk, and swimming.  For walk and run the data captured was okay for duration, calories, distance, number of steps, pace, heart rate. and nighttime monitoring sleep was interesting.  Then I took it for laps in the pool, and that is when it did not do well.  The ad shows person swimming in pool, but I don't believe it can survive in the pool.  because the watch died.

reviews.title: A sluggish, inaccurate, subpar sports/health smart watch.
reviews.text:
I needed a backup sports smart watch for my Apple Watch. Let's check out this Veryfit watch.

What works:
Charged up in about 2-3 hours from 60% battery level.
Easy to install the Veryfit app and pair with my old iPhone 12 Pro.
Has many of the basic sports watch functions: stopwatch, alarm, timer, along with biometrics: Heart rate, Blood oxygen monitoring, Stress level, Breath training, Sleep, etc.
Big, easy to read watch face, only a bit narrower than my 44mm Apple Watch.
A variety of watch faces to download.
Nice, soft, comfortable watch straps.

What don't work:
Once linked up to my iPhone, app takes some effort to set up.
Can only receive notifications and phone call alerts, no relay of calls.
Heart rate monitor is erratic with a wide range of random readings. My resting heart rate is 56bpm yet this watch measures a range of 62 to 125bpm at various times.
Requires about 25s to get each biometric reading.
What's up with the 3-2-1 countdown to start each exercise? Start/Stop is what most users prefer.
Perhaps the Android version works better?
Don't expect too much from this $34.99 smart watch (after -30% off coupon on Feb. 19, 2023) but there are other smart watches in this price range that function better.

reviews.title: works to add security to fitbit watch band
reviews.text:
Good. I have been using these bands as extra security on my fitbit bands since I first got a fitbit dating back to 2015. I have had several styles since then, and I always make sure to have a pack of these security bands on hand. First it was because the fitbit band was just a pressure close band that easily popped off, then it was a matter of keeping a regular watch band style extra secure. These work on the regular width bands.

When I was ready to buy more of these security bands, I was about to buy some that are made for fitbit, but then I realized that those have a protrusion for locking into the band. I really just wanted something to slip on with no need to make use of the band holes to secure it. These are exactly what I wanted. Nice variety of colors.

reviews.title: My Approach S6 -- Near Perfection!
reviews.text: Very pleased with the quality of this band replacement for my Approach S6 band and the ease of installation. Having a buddy helps, but the installation is easily accomplished on your own within a matter of minutes.  The new band is more flexible than the original which, IMHO, is a plus.  What I can't say is that the original band might have stiffened with age.  Who knows and it doesn't matter.  The screwdrivers are also an awesome design -- the handle ends have a hex shape you can stick in your drill if your hands are weak.  The only minuscule minus is that the retainer bar/strap is a tad large as others have stated, but problem solved by reusing the retainer strap from the original band. VERY pleased with this purchase.

reviews.title: Some Good Aspects, But Several Annoying Issues
reviews.text:
This CNPGD Smart Watch Fitness Tracker had the potential to be really great. In terms of appearance, the body of the watch combined with the band are sleek and attractive. The watch is available in 4 different color schemes (Black, Pink, Red, and Blue/Silver) and each looks aesthetically pleasing. I received the Black one and really liked the clean lines and modern design. The band fits around the wrist comfortably as well.

Before trying to turn the watch on, I decided to charge it fully overnight. I followed the instructions and photos on the instruction sheet and tried to remove the band from the watch's charging port end. Unfortunately, it was so difficult to remove that I thought I was going to damage the watch or the band at some point as I worked up a sweat trying to pry/pull it apart. I finally managed to insert the lip of a plastic spoon into a sliver of space I had gotten between the band and the watch. I kept working at it until I was finally able to get the band off. I really do not look forward to repeating this process.

Once the watch was charged, I downloaded the WearFit 2.0 App and after a couple of tries, was able to pair my phone with the watch. There are some annoying aspects about the App. In one area, I was able to specify customary units such as pounds and feet versus the default kilograms and centimeters, but in another area, I was unable to make the change. When using the watch, as I started to toggle through the functions, I realized that there was no way to toggle backwards. So if I swiped past my desired function such as the stopwatch feature, I needed to swipe past all the other functions again until I got back to the stopwatch feature. Swiping right or left or pressing the Home button does the same thing and only moves the choice of functions in one direction, forward.

I went out for a long walk and compared the watch's step and distance counter against my phone's fitness app with GPS and a very accurate pocket pedometer. After the walk, my phone fitness app, pedometer, and this watch all gave roughly the same measurement for the mileage. Unfortunately, the step count of the watch was about 2,000 steps off from the other two devices. it's unfortunate that this watch has so many annoying issues as the features such as the BP, O2, and other health trackers are quite handy. I would give this fitness tracker watch more stars if they just made some upgrades/updates to the app and maybe the watch's firmware.

reviews.title: Don't Bother. I have tried 4 of these from them for my Charge 5. Also comes loose from the Fitbit
reviews.text: Headline says it all. My first order, I was hoping it was just a rando, but exchanged, and every single one has the same problem.  Just sloppy construction -- enough out of spec that it might or might not work. I love the band. It's a crying shame it doesn't stay connected to the fitbit. Can't imagine how there could be even a single 5-star -- maybe the first batches were fabricated correctly.  Unfortunately there's nothing as nice or comfortable that I've been able to find for charge-5, so I may end up getting yet a 5th and 6th trial and frankensteining them with parts that do work to get something that works and something I like. I'll let you know

reviews.title: Waste of money
reviews.text: The band keeps stretching out as you wear it. Within a month I couldn’t tighten it anymore.

reviews.title: Yasss!!
reviews.text: No more irritation from original band! I love it and wish I got it sooner

reviews.title: Perfect replacement
reviews.text: Exact replacement and a fraction of the price you would pay if you bought it from the store

reviews.title: Blue is more purple
reviews.text: The blue is more purple than it is blue. The color was mostly true to its display photo though, but just a word of caution in case if anyone really wanted a true blue. As for the band itself, I can’t say much about durability since I just installed it. This band comes with 2 securing bands whereas the original Garmin comes with 1. I also feel like the band doesn’t meet flush with the watch face as well as the original Garmin but it’s not bad. This band is also a little stiffer than the floppy Garmin band, and I think I actually like the feel of this stiffer band.

reviews.title: Fits 45s perfectly
reviews.text: Worked perfectly as a replacement band for the 45s. Not sure why other reviewers are having issues because it’s great!

reviews.title: I wanted the teal specifically
reviews.text: I replaced my watch band as soon as they came and it's been on since summer with no complaints.  I appreciate the fact that they send a little screwdriver with the set, it's so helpful not having to find something that small on your own.  Nice product!

title: WONMILLE Posh Leather Bands Compatible with Fitbit Charge 5 Bands for Women, Handmade Multilayer Wrap Bracelets Jewelry Strap Wristband Replacement for Fitbit Charge 5 Fitness Tracker

features: Compatible Models: This WONMILLE Top Grain Leather Bands Only Compatible with Fitbit Charge 5 Fitness Tracker Watch Bands.
Superior Materials: Posh leather band, won't pilling or peeling off, with high quality solid stainless steel links for easy resizing. More stylish and attractive than standard versa bands.

Easy to Resize: Suitable for 5.3-7.5 inch wrists. Three folding clasp type links allow rapid and secure resizing without the need for tools. Simply remove a folding clasp to resize the band, no tools needed!

Unique Design: Vintage elegance is at hand with this design to compliment most wrist. This Watch band is perfect for matching with jewelry or clothing. Charge 5 band will give your look an edge with its perfect fusion of leather and stainless steel.

Perfect gift: Available in multiple colors, this multilayer leather bracelet is excellent gift idea. Delight mom on Mother's Day, your girlfriend for Valentine's Day, your daughter for Christmas or best friend on her birthday.
Patents Item: Original design, we have applied this design patent, please do not copy.

reviews.title: Pretty but...
reviews.text: I've had this for less than 2 weeks and the brackets around the black straps have patinaed and the black covering on the "leather" straps have started to sluff off. Not impressed

reviews.title: Love this Fitbit band!
reviews.text: This band really dressed my Fitbit up and is comfortable to wear. I purchased it in black as well. I like that you can easily take out links to make the band smaller as I have a very small wrist. Great price too!

reviews.title: Picture is better
reviews.text: Band is large. Clip comes undone. You have to be very careful when wearing.
